emails.subject: heat rates
emails.body: Quick question: Do the heat rates you have in your spreadsheet change with the addition of chillers? We are looking at an LM6000 deal in Entergy in which the customer would add chillers (no cost to us) and I wonder if this changes heat rate. Thanks for your time, Claire X 3 0390 Forwarded by Berney C Aucoin/HOU/ECT on 03/28/2000 10:43 AM Benjamin Rogers 03/27/2000 09:48 AM

emails.subject: Re: heat rates
emails.body: Re: heat rates Basically, having chillers in the project (running at full load) will increase the Heat Rate by approximately 50 degrees (ie. if HR is 10,465 w/o chillers to 10,409 w chillers). Let me know if you have any questions. Thanks Ben
